segments.text: And when Rachel saw that she bare Jacob no children, Rachel envied her sister;
Give me children, or else I die.
segments.speaker: Rachel

segments.text: And Jacob's anger was kindled against Rachel:
Am I in God's stead, who hath withheld from thee the fruit of the womb?
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: Behold my maid Bilhah, go in unto her; and she shall bear upon my knees, that I may also have children by her.
segments.speaker: Rachel

footnotes: have children by her: Heb. be built by her

segments.text: And she gave him Bilhah her handmaid to wife: and Jacob went in unto her.
And Bilhah conceived, and bare Jacob a son.
God hath judged me, and hath also heard my voice, and hath given me a son: therefore called she his name Dan.
segments.speaker: Rachel

segments.text: And Reuben went in the days of wheat harvest, and found mandrakes in the field, and brought them unto his mother Leah.
Give me, I pray thee, of thy son's mandrakes.
segments.speaker: Rachel

segments.text: Is it a small matter that thou hast taken my husband? and wouldest thou take away my son's mandrakes also?
segments.speaker: Leah

segments.text: Therefore he shall lie with thee to night for thy son's mandrakes.
segments.speaker: Rachel

segments.text: And God remembered Rachel, and God hearkened to her, and opened her womb.
And it came to pass, when Rachel had born Joseph, that Jacob said unto Laban,
Send me away, that I may go unto mine own place, and to my country.
segments.speaker: Jacob

segments.text: And Jacob took him rods of green poplar, and of the hazel and chesnut tree; and pilled white strakes in them, and made the white appear which was in the rods.
